root_text.an4.107:1.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro mūsikūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.107:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo gādhaṁ kattā hoti no vasitā?
root_text.an4.107:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.107:2.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.107:2.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.107:2.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo gādhaṁ kattā hoti, no vasitā.
root_text.an4.107:2.6: Seyyathāpi sā, bhikkhave, mūsikā gādhaṁ kattā, no vasitā;
root_text.an4.107:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo vasitā hoti, no gādhaṁ kattā?
root_text.an4.107:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ na pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.107:3.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.107:3.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.107:3.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo vasitā hoti, no gādhaṁ kattā.
root_text.an4.107:3.6: Seyyathāpi sā, bhikkhave, mūsikā vasitā hoti, no gādhaṁ kattā;
root_text.an4.107:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo neva gādhaṁ kattā hoti no vasitā?
root_text.an4.107:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ na pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.107:4.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.107:4.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.107:4.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo neva gādhaṁ kattā hoti, no vasitā.
root_text.an4.107:4.6: Seyyathāpi sā, bhikkhave, mūsikā neva gādhaṁ kattā hoti, no vasitā;
root_text.an4.107:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo gādhaṁ kattā ca hoti vasitā ca?
root_text.an4.107:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.107:5.3: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.107:5.4: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.107:5.5: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo gādhaṁ kattā ca hoti vasitā ca.
root_text.an4.107:5.6: Seyyathāpi sā, bhikkhave, mūsikā gādhaṁ kattā ca hoti vasitā ca;
root_text.an4.107:5.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro mūsikūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.107:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of mice.
translation_text.an4.107:1.3: One makes a hole but doesn’t live in it,
translation_text.an4.107:1.4: one lives in a hole but doesn’t make it,
translation_text.an4.107:1.5: one neither makes a hole nor lives in it, and
translation_text.an4.107:1.6: one both makes a hole and lives in it.
translation_text.an4.107:1.7: These are the four kinds of mice.
translation_text.an4.107:1.8: In the same way, these four individuals similar to mice are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.107:1.10: One makes a hole but doesn’t live in it,
translation_text.an4.107:1.11: one lives in a hole but doesn’t make it,
translation_text.an4.107:1.12: one neither makes a hole nor lives in it, and
translation_text.an4.107:1.13: one both makes a hole and lives in it.
translation_text.an4.107:2.1: And how does an individual make a hole but not live in it?
translation_text.an4.107:2.2: It’s when an individual memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an4.107:2.3: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.107:2.4: But they don’t really understand: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an4.107:2.5: That’s how an individual makes a hole but doesn’t live in it.
translation_text.an4.107:2.6: That individual is like a mouse that makes a hole but doesn’t live in it, I say.
translation_text.an4.107:3.1: And how does an individual live in a hole but not make it?
translation_text.an4.107:3.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t memorize the teaching …
translation_text.an4.107:3.4: But they really understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.107:4.1: And how does an individual neither make a hole nor live in it?
translation_text.an4.107:4.2: It’s when an individual doesn’t memorize the teaching …
translation_text.an4.107:4.4: Nor do they really understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.107:5.1: And how does an individual both make a hole and live in it?
translation_text.an4.107:5.2: It’s when an individual memorizes the teaching …
translation_text.an4.107:5.4: And they really understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.107:5.8: These four individuals similar to mice are found in the world.”
